overview: A stateful orchestration layer for coding agents (like Cursor Agent CLI) designed to provide long-term memory, modular code ownership, and multi-agent coordination. It utilizes native Cursor primitives (.mdc files) and a custom metadata structure (.ace/) to implement a write-back loop, iterative testing (RALPH), and asynchronous agent communication.

requirements.title: Core Infrastructure and Project Initialization
requirements.description: The system must establish a standardized file structure to store agent metadata, ownership records, and session logs.
success_criteria: Command 'ace init' successfully creates the .ace/ directory and sub-folders (mail, sessions, decisions).
The system generates a valid AGENTS.md in the project root as a global memory file.
The .ace/agents.yaml and .ace/ownership.yaml files are initialized with correct versioning schemas.

sub_requirements.title: Agent Registry Management
sub_requirements.description: Maintain a central registry of all active agents, their roles, and contact details.
success_criteria: Command 'ace agent create' adds a new entry to agents.yaml with unique ID and email.
Command 'ace agent list' accurately displays all registered agents and their status.

requirements.title: Ownership Registry
requirements.description: A mechanism to map specific code modules or file paths to dedicated Agent Teams to ensure contextual consistency.
success_criteria: The system resolves ownership using a longest-prefix-match algorithm for file paths.
Command 'ace who <file>' returns the correct agent ID assigned to that module.
Ownership conflicts are prevented by allowing only one primary owner per file.

requirements.title: Dynamic Context Builder
requirements.description: Compose a specific 'memory slice' for each agent call based on the task type and module ownership.
success_criteria: The builder successfully aggregates _global.mdc, the specific <role>.mdc, and recent ADRs into a single prompt prefix.
Task-specific framing (implement, review, debug) is correctly prepended to the agent prompt.
Command 'ace context show' outputs the exact string that would be injected into the LLM.

requirements.title: Write-back Pipeline
requirements.description: An automated loop where the agent's output is analyzed to extract lessons, strategies, and pitfalls to update its long-term memory (.mdc).
success_criteria: The system extracts [str-XXX] and [mis-XXX] tags from agent output using a reflection prompt.
The .mdc files are updated incrementally without overwriting existing manual notes or structure.
Helpful/Harmful counters are correctly incremented/decremented based on task success or failure.

requirements.title: RALPH Loop Engine
requirements.description: An iterative execution motor (Reasoning, Action, Learning, Progress, Halt) that runs the agent until tests pass.
success_criteria: The engine automatically triggers a re-run if the specified test command fails.
The system halts execution and alerts the user if 'max_iterations' is reached.
Each iteration includes a 'Learning' phase where the failure reason is written back to the agent's memory before the next attempt.

requirements.title: Multi-Agent Coordination and Messaging
requirements.description: Enable asynchronous communication and consensus-building between different specialized agents.
success_criteria: Agent Mail system correctly stores messages in .ace/mail/<agent-id>/inbox as Markdown files.
The system detects cross-module dependencies and triggers a 'Consensus Protocol' debate.
A neutral 'arch-agent' or LLM-referee can resolve debates or escalate to a human after 3 rounds.

sub_requirements.title: Token Consumption Modes
sub_requirements.description: Control operational costs by limiting context and debate depth.
success_criteria: Low mode disables asynchronous debates and minimizes context injection.
High mode enables full multi-agent audits and proactive refactoring suggestions.

requirements.title: UI/UX Orchestration (Google Stitch)
requirements.description: Integration with Google Stitch for AI-driven design-to-code workflows.
success_criteria: The system can generate a 'Vibe Design' prompt for Google Stitch based on project constraints.
Command 'ace ui sync <url>' successfully imports Tailwind/Flutter code from a Stitch canvas into the local codebase.

requirements.title: Quality Assurance and TDD Framework
requirements.description: The orchestrator itself must be developed and validated using strict TDD and DX/AX metrics.
success_criteria: 100% pass rate on unit tests for Context Builder and Ownership Registry logic.
Automated CI/CD pipeline prevents merges if test coverage on critical modules drops below 90%.
Agentic Experience (AX) is validated by a 'Context Relevance Score' measuring agent success rate with vs. without ACE context.
